text: How does Madelung-Psi handle the N=0 point at x=0? Does direct (N, phi) blow up? What regularization is needed? Are periodic-vs-Dirichlet boundary conditions an issue?

method: Strang split-step Fourier on Psi (Madelung-Psi, periodic basis), kappa=-1, defocusing. Also cross-checked with Strang-split CN-Fourier (M3) at dt=5e-3 -- same equation, different time scheme.

rationale: Psi-formulation tolerates N->0 fine (no special handling needed; min |Psi|^2 reaches 1e-10). Mass conserved to 1e-11. BUT density is corrupted from T=0.01 onward (Linf=0.74 already) and phase jump drifts from -pi to -2.11 rad by T=4. Root cause discovered: tanh(-L)=-1, tanh(+L)=+1 -- a jump of 2 across the periodic boundary -- triggers Gibbs ringing that propagates inward and destroys the dark soliton structure. The prompt's note 'periodicity satisfied to 1e-13' is WRONG: tanh is anti-periodic on [-L,L], not periodic; |Psi(L)-Psi(-L)|=2.0.
M1 (and CN cross-check M3) both show same boundary-Gibbs failure. Need to test the direct (N,phi) formulation (E2) and a method with correct BC handling (E3).

method: Direct (N, phi) operator split, dN/dt=-(Nv)_x with v=phi_x; dphi/dt=-1/2 v^2 - Q(N) + kappa*(N-1); Q(N)=-(sqrt N)_xx/(2 sqrt N) using N<-max(N, eps) regularization

rationale: ALL three eps values blow up within T<0.04. Larger eps delays but does not prevent blow-up. Mechanism: quantum pressure Q ~ -(sqrt N)_xx/(2 sqrt N) requires sqrt(N) to be smooth and bounded away from zero. At x=0 (exact density node) sqrt(N) has a kink (Psi=tanh has Psi'(0)~1 but sqrt(Psi^2)=|Psi|=|tanh| is C^0 only -- it has a derivative discontinuity). Floor-regularization N<-max(N,eps) introduces a non-smooth corner -- (sqrt N)_xx develops a Dirac-like spike there, kicking phi violently and creating runaway velocity. Increasing eps just changes WHEN it blows up, not WHETHER. Direct (N, phi) is FUNDAMENTALLY INCOMPATIBLE with phase singularities (vortex/node points).

E2 confirms direct (N,phi) cannot handle the N=0 node regardless of eps choice. Pivot to fixing E1's boundary-mismatch issue: replace periodic Fourier basis with anti-periodic Fourier (k_n=(2n+1)pi/(2L)) which matches Psi(-L)=-Psi(L).

method: Anti-periodic Fourier split-step on Psi: U=exp(-i pi x/(2L))*Psi is periodic; evolve U with shifted wavenumbers k_n+pi/(2L) for the kinetic operator. Same Strang split as M1 otherwise.

rationale: Anti-periodic Fourier gives MACHINE-PRECISION handling of the phase singularity: rho(0)=2e-26 (exact zero), min_N=3e-32 (literally zero in fp64), phase jump=pi to 7 digits. At short time (T=0.01) density error is 6.5e-5 -- four orders of magnitude better than periodic M1's 0.74. Long-time density evolution (Linf=2.79 at T=4) reflects real PDE dynamics: tanh(x) is NOT a stationary solution of the bare equation i Psi_t = -1/2 Psi_xx + kappa(|Psi|^2-1)Psi unless one uses the proper dark-soliton stretching tanh(x/sqrt(2*|kappa|)). M4's value is preserving the SINGULARITY STRUCTURE -- node + pi phase jump -- not freezing the profile. This directly answers the prompt's boundary-condition question.

Three findings give complete knowledge: (1) Psi-formulation natively handles N=0 (M1, M3, M4 all reach min_N<1e-9); (2) direct (N,phi) blows up at all eps values regardless -- not a regularization-strength issue; (3) periodic Fourier introduces a Gibbs-jump-2 boundary artifact that destroys the dark soliton at T<<1; anti-periodic Fourier is the correct fix. Stop iterating; write up findings.
